pattern: A single left-to-right scan with a block-comment flag strips C-style line and block comments while joining lines that a block comment spans.
hints: Track one boolean: are we currently inside a block comment that opened on an earlier line?
When not in a block comment, look for the first '//' or '/*' on the line; line comments end the line, block comments switch on the flag.

Scan character by character. Outside a block: '//' skips the rest of the line, '/*' enters block mode, otherwise append the char to a buffer. Inside a block: only '*/' exits. Flush the buffer to the output when a line ends and you are not inside a block; emit only non-empty buffers.

edge_inputs.description: block comment spanning many lines joins surrounding code
block comment merges across implicit newlines into one output line
the '/*/' sequence does not close the block it opens
line comment removes trailing code only
block comment fully consumes a line so it is dropped
single empty line produces empty output
slash that is not a comment opener is preserved
block end and a new line comment on the same line

stress.description: random C-like lines with scattered comment tokens, occasionally crossing lines
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    toks = ['a', 'b', 'c', ' ', ';', '{', '}', 'x=1', '//c', '/*', '*/', '/']
    lines = []
    for _ in range(n):
        length = rng.randrange(0, 9)
        parts = [rng.choice(toks) for _ in range(length)]
        line = ''.join(parts)[:80]
        lines.append(line)
    # ensure any open block eventually closes: append a closer line
    lines.append('*/end')
    return [lines[:100]]
stress.note: comment tokens may straddle lines; trailing closer guarantees every open block closes
